questionHtml:
Let R α be the matrix representing a rotation, counter-clockwise (anticlockwise) about the origin, through an angle of α.

Write down R 2α as a 2×2 matrix.
markschemeHtml:
cos 2α   -sin 2αsin 2α     cos 2α          A1A1

 

Note: Award A1 for selecting the correct matrix, A1 for substituting 2α into a rotation matrix

Award A1A0 for clockwise rotation. These marks can be awarded independently. Condone the use of a different symbol.

[2 marks]

meta.title: Write down R 2α as a 2×2 matrix. Calculate Rα×Rα. Use your answers from part (a) and part (b) to explain why sin 2α=2 sin α cos α. show that cos 2α=1-2 sin2 α.
breadcrumbLabels: AHL 5.14—Setting up a DE, solve by separating variables
AHL 3.8—Unit circle, Pythag identity, solving trig equations graphically
